hero_subtitle: SFTY supports sovereign data protection, role-based access, audit logging, and phased deployment to meet stringent government requirements for digital asset supervision.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles

problem_title: Data protection challenges slow government deployments
problem_description: Government teams face operational friction when sovereign data protections are not fully integrated into deployment infrastructure. This creates risks around access governance, audit logging, and compliance with internal procurement and security requirements.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY integrates role-based access, audit-grade logging, data sovereignty controls and phased implementation features to align with government procurement and security policies for reliable supervisory deployment.
workflow_title: From access policy to audit log control
workflow_description: SFTY structures access governance, role-based permissions and audit logs to preserve evidence and support secure sovereign data handling throughout the deployment lifecycle.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. A sovereign control layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides regulators a secure, auditable supervisory layer that connects fragmented data with controlled access, preserving evidence and supporting sovereign deployment requirements.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY delivers secure deployment with role-based access, evidence-grade logging, and a procurement-safe architecture to meet stringent government data protection standards.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How does SFTY support sovereign data protection in deployment

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY support sovereign data protection?
faq_1_answer: SFTY supports sovereign data protection through secure deployment options, controlled data handling, role-based permissions and segregation aligned with regulatory and jurisdictional requirements.
faq_2_question: How is access governance managed in SFTY?
faq_2_answer: SFTY uses role-based access controls with user permissions and audit logs to manage secure and governed access to sensitive supervisory data and workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit logs and evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit logs, maintains case histories and records supervisory decisions to support review readiness and defensible operational workflows.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: SFTY can integrate modularly with public-sector registries, reporting systems and existing supervisory infrastructure to provide a connected operating layer.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing supervisory tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY acts as a supervisory layer connecting provider outputs and workflows without replacing established analytics or compliance tools.
final_title: Review secure deployment and data protection
final_description: Explore how SFTY supports sovereign data handling, role-based access and audit logs for government supervisory infrastructure.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
